paradāresu.meaning: among the wives of others; with regard to others' wives
breakdown: Locative plural ending: "among, with regard to".

parakaṭukā.meaning: bitter towards others; sharp or harsh to others
parakaṭukā.gram: adjective, compound, plural or feminine form

parakkamaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular (from parakkamati, parā + √kam)
breakdown: Prefix: onward, forward. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To step; parakkama is the stepping forward, strenuous effort.

paralokavajjabhayadassāvine.meaning: seeing fear in faults with regard to the next world; those who see the danger of what is blameworthy for the world beyond
paralokavajjabhayadassāvine.summary: This is a compound: "next world + fault to be shunned + fear + seeing with insight".
paralokavajjabhayadassāvine.gram: adjective, compound, masculine nominative plural (irregular form of paralokavajjabhayadassāvī)
breakdown: World; paraloka is the world beyond, the next life.
Fault, what is to be shunned, blameworthy.
Seeing, one who sees with insight (from √dis "to see").

paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavand.meaning: (truncated) the gain, hospitality, esteem, honour and veneration of others
breakdown: Veneration, homage (the headword breaks off in this element).
paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavand.note: uncertain: the headword is truncated in extraction; it is the beginning of a longer compound enumerating others' gain, hospitality, esteem, honouring and veneration.

paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavan­.meaning: (truncated) the gain, hospitality, esteem, honour and veneration of others
paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavan­.note: uncertain: duplicate of the truncated headword above, ending in a soft-hyphen line-break artifact. See paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavand.

paramattakāmo.meaning: loving oneself beyond (all else); holding the self supremely dear
paramattakāmo.gram: sandhi compound: pronoun + adjective (paraṁ + attakāma), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Beyond, supremely (the niggahīta assimilates to m before a vowel).
Liking, loving, desiring; attakāma is "holding the self dear".

paramattha.meaning: ultimate meaning; highest truth, ultimate reality

paramatthenā.meaning: in the highest sense; from the standpoint of ultimate reality
paramatthenā.gram: masculine noun, instrumental singular of paramattha (with metrical lengthening)
breakdown: Instrumental singular ending: "in the sense of".

paramatthāya.meaning: for the highest benefit; for the supreme welfare
paramatthāya.gram: masculine noun, dative singular of paramattha
breakdown: Dative singular ending: "for the sake of".

parammukhāssa.meaning: of the highest praises; of him with utmost speech (of high praises)
parammukhāssa.gram: sandhi compound, genitive singular (paraṁ + mukha + assa)
breakdown: Of him, of this (genitive singular pronoun).
parammukhāssa.note: uncertain: the lexicon glosses the phrase contextually as "of high praises"; the literal segmentation is "highest + mouth + of".

paramparāya.meaning: by succession; one after the other, by (a lineage of) transmission
paramparāya.gram: adjective/noun, instrumental or dative singular of parampara
breakdown: Other; another — para + para, "one after another". (The m is a junction consonant.)

parantapo.meaning: tormenting others; one who afflicts others

parapariggahite.meaning: what is taken possession of by others; another's property
breakdown: Taken, seized, possessed (past participle of pariggaṇhāti, pari + √gah).

paraparitāpanānuyogamanuyutto.meaning: devoted to the practice of tormenting others; involved in afflicting another
breakdown: Application to, devotion to, practice. (The long ā at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)
Practising, engaged in, involved in (past participle of anuyuñjati).

parappavādaṁ.meaning: the doctrines of others; others' speech, opposing teachings
breakdown: Speech, doctrine, disputation (pa + √vad; the doubled pp is compound sandhi doubling).

paravisaṁvādanakakāyavacīpayogasa.meaning: (truncated) the effort of body and speech that deceives another
paravisaṁvādanakakāyavacīpayogasa.note: uncertain: this headword is truncated in extraction; see the full entry paravisaṁvādanakakāyavacīpayogasamuṭṭhāpikā.

paravisaṁvādanakakāyavacīpayogasamuṭṭhāp.meaning: occasioning effort of body and speech to deceive another (a definition of false speech)
paravisaṁvādanakakāyavacīpayogasamuṭṭhāp.summary: This is a long analytical compound: "other + deceiving + by means of body and speech + causing, occasioning".
breakdown: Deceiving, lying; someone who deceives (vi + saṁ + √vad).
Means or effort of body and speech (kāya + vacī + payoga).
Causing, occasioning, setting in motion (saṁ + ud + √ṭhā, causative).

paravittūpakaraṇaṁ.meaning: the property and means of support of others; others' acquired possessions and livelihood
breakdown: Means of support, equipment. (The long ū at the junction arises by vowel coalescence.)

paravādappamaddanaṁ.meaning: the crushing of others' doctrines; refuting opposing teachings
breakdown: Crushing, overpowering (pa + √madd; the doubled pp is compound sandhi doubling).

pare.gram: pronoun, masculine nominative plural or locative singular of para

paribbājakā.meaning: wandering mendicants; pilgrims, religious wanderers (outside the Buddha's order)
paribbājakā.gram: masculine agent noun, nominative plural of paribbājaka (pari + √vaj)
breakdown: To go; paribbajati means to wander about as a religious mendicant.

paribhūto.gram: past participle of paribhavati (pari + √bhū), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Around; here with a pejorative force.
To be; paribhavati means to despise, treat with contempt.

pariccattaṁ.gram: past participle of pariccajati (pari + √caj), neuter/accusative singular
breakdown: Completely. (The doubled cc arises by assimilation.)
To give up; pariccajati means to renounce, sacrifice.

paricitā.meaning: accumulated; practised, attended to, made familiar
paricitā.gram: past participle of paricināti (pari + √ci), plural or feminine form
breakdown: To gather; paricita means accumulated, well practised, accustomed.

paricārayamānaṁ.meaning: indulging in; amusing oneself, being waited upon
paricārayamānaṁ.gram: present participle of paricāreti (pari + √car, causative), accusative singular
breakdown: To move; the causative paricāreti means to amuse oneself, indulge the senses.

paridahissati.meaning: will put on; will dress oneself
paridahissati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of paridahati (pari + √dhā)
breakdown: To put; paridahati means to put on, clothe oneself.
To lament; paridevati means to wail, cry out.

paridevehi.gram: masculine noun, instrumental/ablative plural of parideva

paridevitattaṁ.meaning: the state of lamentation; the condition of crying and bewailing

paridevo.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (from paridevati, pari + √dev)
breakdown: To lament; parideva is wailing, lamentation.
paridevo.goenka: Parideva, lamentation, appears in the exposition of the First Noble Truth in the Mahāsatipaṭṭhānasutta studied in the tradition: birth is suffering, and so are sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief and despair (soka-parideva-dukkha-domanassa-upāyāsā). It names the audible wailing born of loss — one of the forms in which dukkha manifests and which the meditator learns to observe with equanimity.

paridhassati.meaning: will put on; will dress oneself (variant of paridahissati)
paridhassati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of paridahati (pari + √dhā)
breakdown: To put; paridahati means to put on, dress.
To shine, illuminate; paridīpeti means to explain, make clear.

pariggaṇheyyāsi.meaning: you should embrace; you would seize, take hold of, examine
pariggaṇheyyāsi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular optative of pariggaṇhāti (pari + √gah)
breakdown: To take, seize; pariggaṇhāti means to embrace, take possession of, examine.
Second person singular optative ending: "you should".
To hide; parigūhati means to conceal.

pariharissāmī.meaning: I will attend to; I will look after, take care of, preserve
pariharissāmī.gram: verb, 1st person singular future of pariharati (pari + √har), with metrical lengthening
breakdown: To carry; pariharati means to carry about, look after, protect.
First person singular future ending: "I will".

parihāniyeva.meaning: just decline; only loss, nothing but diminution
breakdown: To diminish; parihāni is loss, decline.
Just, only (emphatic particle, sandhi form of eva).

parihāpessanti.meaning: they will cause to diminish; they will neglect, let decline
parihāpessanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural future of parihāpeti (causative of parihāyati, pari + √hā)
breakdown: To diminish; the causative parihāpeti means to bring to decline, neglect.
Third person plural future ending: "they will".

parikathā.meaning: roundabout talk; a hint (an improper way of seeking gains)
breakdown: Talk; parikathā is talking around a subject, hinting.
Completely. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To be exhausted; parikkhaya is complete wasting away, destruction.

parikkhayā.meaning: from exhaustion; through the wasting away (of)
parikkhayā.gram: masculine noun, ablative singular of parikkhaya

breakdown: To make; parikkhāra is what is made ready around one — equipment, requisites.

parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano.meaning: one in whom the fetters of becoming are completely exhausted
parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano.summary: This is a compound epithet of the Arahant: "completely exhausted + becoming + fetters".
parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano.goenka: The phrase is a canonical epithet of the Arahant: one in whom the fetters binding to renewed becoming (bhavasaṁyojana) are completely worn away. With the exhaustion of these fetters there is no more fuel for future existence — the goal of the path, full liberation.

parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojanā.meaning: those whose fetters of becoming are completely destroyed

parikkhīṇā.meaning: completely come to an end; destroyed, extinct
parikkhīṇā.gram: past participle of pari + √khī, plural or feminine form
breakdown: Completely, all around. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To be exhausted; parikkhīṇa means utterly exhausted, destroyed.

parimuccanti.meaning: they are completely liberated; they are fully released
parimuccanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, passive of parimuñcati (pari + √muc)
breakdown: To release; parimuccati means to be fully freed.

parimukhaṁ.meaning: in front; around the mouth (the area around the entrance of the nostrils)
parimukhaṁ.goenka: The word occurs in the meditation instruction parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā, "having established awareness in front / around the mouth". In the tradition's practice of ānāpāna this is taken concretely: attention is kept on the area around the entrance of the nostrils, where the breath can be felt as it actually is.

parinibbutā.meaning: completely calmed; fully extinguished, attained to final nibbāna
parinibbutā.gram: past participle of parinibbāti (pari + nir + √vā), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Calmed, quenched, extinguished (past participle of nibbāti, nir + √vā).
